message:
fix(search): CrossEncoderReranker rejects unloaded model instead of silent passthrough (ft-akx00.4.3)

CrossEncoderReranker previously returned documents unchanged when called
without a loaded model, making it indistinguishable from PassthroughReranker.
This violated the API contract of semantic reranking.

Now:
- rerank() returns RerankError::ModelNotLoaded if called before load()
- Explicit load() method validates model path existence
- is_loaded() lets callers check readiness
- Module docs updated to reflect honest capability boundaries

3 new tests: ModelNotLoaded error display, passthrough behavior,
empty-input rejection. All 56 reranker tests pass.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -21,7 +21,7 @@
 {"id":"ft-akx00.4","title":"[EPIC] Replace connector and search placeholder implementations","description":"Own audited placeholders in connector trust/certification and semantic-search quality.

This track covers places where the current code claims verification, certification, or ranking behavior but actually performs a permissive no-op, a silent pass-through, or an always-failing placeholder implementation.","design":"Split the work into two principles:
- trust surfaces must never silently over-accept,
- retrieval quality surfaces must never silently advertise models or ranking that do nothing.

Where a backend is not worth keeping, remove the dead path instead of preserving a fake capability.","acceptance_criteria":"- Connector trust and certification phases do real work or explicitly fail closed.
- Search/reranking/embedder code no longer contains silent no-op model paths.
- Tests cover both configured and unavailable-backend behavior.
- Every concrete child bead under this epic closes with comprehensive unit tests plus a dedicated or explicitly assigned E2E/integration scenario, all executed under the shared verification contract in `ft-akx00.8`.","notes":"Primary files: `crates/frankenterm-core/src/connector_registry.rs`, `crates/frankenterm-core/src/connector_sdk.rs`, `crates/frankenterm-core/src/search/reranker.rs`, `crates/frankenterm-core/src/search/model2vec_embedder.rs`, `crates/frankenterm-core/src/search/fastembed_embedder.rs`.

## Track Verification Emphasis
- fixture-heavy verification with score/evidence capture, certification traces, and explicit unavailable-backend diagnostics.
- Heavy validation runs in this track must capture structured logs, exact command transcripts, and artifact paths.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:28:56.664785Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-03T02:04:29.723041Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["connector","e2e-tests","integration-tests","placeholder-remediation","quality-gate","search","stub-audit","test-logging","user-impact"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-akx00.4","depends_on_id":"ft-akx00","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:28:56.664785Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2744,"issue_id":"ft-akx00.4","author":"jemanuel","text":"This track is partly about security posture and partly about epistemic honesty. A verification or reranking path that quietly does nothing is worse than an explicit "unsupported" error because it invites false confidence.","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:28:57Z"}]}
 {"id":"ft-akx00.4.1","title":"Replace connector transparency verification stub with real verification policy","description":"`crates/frankenterm-core/src/connector_registry.rs::check_transparency()` is currently documented as a stub and effectively treats any non-empty token as verified.

This task owns replacing that permissive placeholder with a real verification policy so connector trust decisions are evidence-based rather than string-presence-based.","design":"1. Define what “transparency” means for this registry: signature chain, log inclusion proof, allowlist, remote attestation, or another verifiable artifact.
2. Implement verification so acceptance is tied to actual proof material.
3. Fail closed when verification material is missing or invalid.
4. Preserve actionable diagnostics so connector authors know why verification failed.","acceptance_criteria":"- A non-empty token alone is no longer sufficient to mark a connector as verified.
- Verification failure modes are explicit and test-covered.
- Comments/docs stop describing the function as a stub.
- Security-sensitive callers cannot accidentally treat placeholder verification as real trust.
- Comprehensive unit tests cover the happy path, edge/boundary cases, explicit error/failure/cancellation modes, and repeat-run stability relevant to this bead.
- A dedicated E2E/integration or explicitly named higher-level suite exercises the user-facing or integration-facing surface touched by this bead, with structured logs, artifacts, and exact execution commands captured under the shared contract in `ft-akx00.8`.","notes":"This task should define the canonical trust primitive that any later certification/integration probe can build on.

## Verification Baseline
- Follow the shared verification and artifact contract in `ft-akx00.8`.
- Record exact commands, artifact paths, and if applicable `rch` worker identity in the completion evidence for this bead.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:31:12.568576Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-03T03:28:23.660686Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["e2e-tests","integration-tests","placeholder-remediation","quality-gate","stub-audit","test-coverage","test-logging","user-impact"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-akx00.4.1","depends_on_id":"ft-akx00.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:31:12.568576Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2755,"issue_id":"ft-akx00.4.1","author":"jemanuel","text":"Silent over-acceptance is the dangerous failure mode here. If the system cannot verify a connector, it must say so plainly and fail closed.","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:31:12Z"},{"id":2781,"issue_id":"ft-akx00.4.1","author":"jemanuel","text":"## Detailed Verification Plan
- Proposed unit tests:
  - valid vs invalid proof material
  - missing material fail-closed behavior
  - diagnostic message stability
- Proposed E2E script: `scripts/e2e_connector_transparency_verification.sh`
  - run good/bad connector registrations through the verification path and snapshot decisions
- Required logs/artifacts:
  - connector id, proof source, decision, failure reason, evidence snapshot with secret-safe redaction","created_at":"2026-04-03T02:04:48Z"},{"id":2800,"issue_id":"ft-akx00.4.1","author":"BeigeBluff","text":"Claimed by BeigeBluff. Implemented fail-closed transparency verification in connector_registry using structured proof tokens plus trusted transparency-root allowlisting, updated trust-policy builder support, and added unit coverage for malformed/missing/untrusted proof material plus reverify/install enforcement. First rch compile exposed a MerkleProof comparability gap for the new token type; fixed by deriving PartialEq/Eq on ProofStep and MerkleProof. Remote verification currently in progress via rch cargo test -p frankenterm-core transparency_valid_token -- --nocapture before widening to connector_registry_transparency_integration and scoped check/clippy.","created_at":"2026-04-03T03:02:35Z"},{"id":2801,"issue_id":"ft-akx00.4.1","author":"WindyDesert","text":"Follow-up from WindyDesert while Agent Mail is intermittently failing:

I found an additional fail-open path after the transparency-token implementation landed: `ConnectorRegistryClient::register_package()` and `reverify_package()` only ran `check_transparency()` when `config.enforce_transparency` was true. If `trust_policy.require_transparency_proof` was true but `enforce_transparency` was false, the policy gate only enforced token presence and did not validate the proof material.

Current working-tree changes on my side add a shared `transparency_verification_required()` gate, route both registration and reverification through it, and add focused unit/integration coverage for the policy-required-proof case plus reverification. Remote verification currently running via `rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenterm-core --test connector_registry_transparency_integration`.
","created_at":"2026-04-03T03:03:26Z"},{"id":2803,"issue_id":"ft-akx00.4.1","author":"WindyDesert","text":"Verification update:

- `rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenterm-core --test connector_registry_transparency_integration` passed on worker `vmi1152480` at 2026-04-03T03:13Z.
- Result: 2/2 integration tests passed (`connector_registry_transparency_integration_policy_required_proof_fails_closed`, `connector_registry_transparency_integration_policy_required_proof_accepts_valid_token`).
- Remote runtime reported `Finished test profile [unoptimized + debuginfo] target(s) in 5m 01s`; end-to-end RCH wall time was ~416.6s including sync/artifact roundtrip.

I am continuing with additional `rch` cargo gates from here.
","created_at":"2026-04-03T03:13:32Z"},{"id":2804,"issue_id":"ft-akx00.4.1","author":"WindyDesert","text":"Workspace verification update:

- `rch exec -- cargo check --workspace --all-targets` reached a non-bead blocker in `frankenterm/window/src/os/xdg_desktop_portal.rs:116` on worker `vmi1152480`.
- Error: `CachedAppearance::Some(appearance)` expected `Appearance` but the current code passes `&Appearance`; compiler suggests `*appearance`.
- The run also emitted unrelated warnings under `frankenterm/window/src/connection.rs` and `frankenterm/window/src/os/x11/window.rs`.

This means the full workspace gate is currently red for reasons outside `connector_registry`. I am following up with a crate-scoped clippy pass on `frankenterm-core` to keep verification relevant to this bead.
","created_at":"2026-04-03T03:21:01Z"},{"id":2805,"issue_id":"ft-akx00.4.1","author":"BeigeBluff","text":"Coordination fallback note: Agent Mail health is green, but `fetch_inbox`, `list_agents`, and `send_message` are still intermittently failing with transient DB errors on my side. I reviewed WindyDesert's bead comments and confirmed the current tree already includes the shared `transparency_verification_required()` path plus the new integration surface at `crates/frankenterm-core/tests/connector_registry_transparency_integration.rs`, so I am avoiding overlapping code edits and focusing on scoped verification + evidence capture. Current evidence: my `rch` bundle at `artifacts/placeholder-remediation/ft-akx00.4.1/scoped-verification/20260403T031146Z` already passed `cargo test -p frankenterm-core --lib transparency -- --nocapture` on worker `vmi1227854`; the integration test step is still running there.","created_at":"2026-04-03T03:26:15Z"},{"id":2806,"issue_id":"ft-akx00.4.1","author":"WindyDesert","text":"Additional verification update:

- `rch exec -- cargo clippy -p frankenterm-core --tests -- -D warnings` is currently blocked by unrelated lint debt in `frankenterm/dynamic/derive/src/attr.rs:29` (`clippy::collapsible_match`).
- `rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenterm-core transparency -- --nocapture` did not reach a transparency-specific failure; it stopped on pre-existing compile errors in `crates/frankenterm-core/tests/asupersync_spike.rs` (Select/Unpin and pattern-shape issues around lines 291-322).

Net: the new transparency integration lane is green, but broader crate/workspace verification remains blocked by unrelated existing failures outside this bead.
","created_at":"2026-04-03T03:27:46Z"},{"id":2807,"issue_id":"ft-akx00.4.1","author":"BeigeBluff","text":"Verification update: the scoped `rch` bundle at `artifacts/placeholder-remediation/ft-akx00.4.1/scoped-verification/20260403T031146Z` now has both focused test legs green on worker `vmi1227854`.

- `rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenterm-core --lib transparency -- --nocapture` passed in 575648 ms.
- `rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenterm-core --test connector_registry_transparency_integration -- --nocapture` passed in 300588 ms (2/2 tests green).
- The same bundle is still running `rch exec -- cargo check -p frankenterm-core --lib` followed by the two crate-scoped clippy legs.

Agent Mail remains degraded here: health is green, but tool/resource reads fail with `no such table: projects`, so beads comments remain my fallback coordination channel until that MCP state is repaired.","created_at":"2026-04-03T03:28:23Z"}]}
 {"id":"ft-akx00.4.2","title":"Replace connector certification integration-probe stub with an executable probe stage","description":"The certification flow in `crates/frankenterm-core/src/connector_sdk.rs` still labels phase 6 as an “Integration probe (stub)” and auto-passes it unless an earlier phase failed.

This task owns turning that phase into a real probe that validates connector integration claims instead of rubber-stamping them.","design":"1. Define the minimal executable probe contract: startup, schema compatibility, round-trip request/response, safety policy handshake, or similar.
2. Reuse the real trust primitive from transparency verification where relevant.
3. Ensure the probe produces structured evidence that can be surfaced in certification output.
4. Remove auto-pass behavior for unexecuted probes.","acceptance_criteria":"- Phase 6 is no longer a stub or unconditional pass-through.
- Certification output distinguishes probe success, probe failure, and probe not-run states explicitly.
- Tests cover happy path, connector misbehavior, and missing prerequisites.
- Docs/comments accurately describe the certification pipeline.
- Comprehensive unit tests cover the happy path, edge/boundary cases, explicit error/failure/cancellation modes, and repeat-run stability relevant to this bead.
- A dedicated E2E/integration or explicitly named higher-level suite exercises the user-facing or integration-facing surface touched by this bead, with structured logs, artifacts, and exact execution commands captured under the shared contract in `ft-akx00.8`.","notes":"Coordinate probe scope with registry trust work so the project does not end up with two incompatible notions of “verified connector”.

## Verification Baseline
- Follow the shared verification and artifact contract in `ft-akx00.8`.
- Record exact commands, artifact paths, and if applicable `rch` worker identity in the completion evidence for this bead.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:31:13.090086Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-03T02:04:48.654735Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["e2e-tests","integration-tests","placeholder-remediation","quality-gate","stub-audit","test-coverage","test-logging","user-impact"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-akx00.4.2","depends_on_id":"ft-akx00.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:31:13.090086Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-akx00.4.2","depends_on_id":"ft-akx00.4.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:33:16.565626Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2756,"issue_id":"ft-akx00.4.2","author":"jemanuel","text":"The optimal outcome is a certification stage that creates real confidence rather than extra ceremony. A fake probe is worse than no probe because it teaches everyone to trust green checkmarks that mean nothing.","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:31:13Z"},{"id":2782,"issue_id":"ft-akx00.4.2","author":"jemanuel","text":"## Detailed Verification Plan
- Proposed unit tests:
  - certification phase-state transitions
  - probe evidence capture and not-run/fail/success outcomes
  - auto-pass removal regression coverage
- Proposed E2E script: `scripts/e2e_connector_certification_probe.sh`
  - execute a full certification run against a fake or controlled connector implementation
- Required logs/artifacts:
  - phase number, probe command, durations, evidence bundle, summary JSON","created_at":"2026-04-03T02:04:48Z"}]}

This task owns making reranking real or explicitly unavailable; a silent identity transform is not acceptable if the API claims semantic reranking.","design":"1. Decide the supported reranking backend(s) and configuration story.

The anti-goal is silent semantic theater.

- Required logs/artifacts:

 {"id":"ft-akx00.4.4","title":"Rationalize semantic embedder paths: implement Model2Vec or retire it, and remove FastEmbed sentinel behavior","description":"Two audited search backends currently behave like placeholders:
- `Model2VecEmbedder::embed()` always returns `ModelNotFound` because the path is a stub.
- the legacy FastEmbed constructor in `fastembed_embedder.rs` can create a sentinel object that only fails later when real embedding is attempted.

This task owns making backend availability explicit and removing placeholder constructor behavior.","design":"1. Decide whether Model2Vec is worth supporting in this project; if yes, implement it fully, and if not, remove/de-register the dead path.
2. Replace FastEmbed sentinel construction with eager validation or an explicit unavailable backend state.
3. Ensure backend enumeration, config parsing, and runtime behavior all agree on what is actually supported.
4. Add tests for configured, missing-model, and unsupported-backend cases.","acceptance_criteria":"- No shipping embedder path advertises support while only failing via a placeholder stub or late sentinel.
- Backend discovery and runtime execution agree on supported models.
- Model2Vec is either genuinely implemented or intentionally retired from the advertised surface.
- Tests cover eager validation and explicit failure semantics.
- Comprehensive unit tests cover the happy path, edge/boundary cases, explicit error/failure/cancellation modes, and repeat-run stability relevant to this bead.
- A dedicated E2E/integration or explicitly named higher-level suite exercises the user-facing or integration-facing surface touched by this bead, with structured logs, artifacts, and exact execution commands captured under the shared contract in `ft-akx00.8`.","notes":"This is a good place to simplify the search surface aggressively under the repo’s no-backwards-compatibility policy.

## Verification Baseline
- Follow the shared verification and artifact contract in `ft-akx00.8`.
- Record exact commands, artifact paths, and if applicable `rch` worker identity in the completion evidence for this bead.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:31:14.136059Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-03T02:04:49.128915Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["e2e-tests","integration-tests","placeholder-remediation","quality-gate","stub-audit","test-coverage","test-logging","user-impact"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-akx00.4.4","depends_on_id":"ft-akx00.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:31:14.136059Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2758,"issue_id":"ft-akx00.4.4","author":"jemanuel","text":"The sophisticated move here may be subtraction: one honest embedder backend is better than multiple fake ones that widen the surface area without delivering capability.","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:31:14Z"},{"id":2784,"issue_id":"ft-akx00.4.4","author":"jemanuel","text":"## Detailed Verification Plan
- Proposed unit tests:
  - backend enumeration and availability agreement
  - eager validation for configured/missing/unsupported backends
  - explicit failure semantics for retired or unavailable models
- Proposed E2E script: `scripts/e2e_semantic_backend_selection.sh`
  - start semantic search/index flows under supported and unsupported backend configurations
- Required logs/artifacts:
  - backend chosen, model path or identifier, validation result, configuration snapshot, artifact dir","created_at":"2026-04-03T02:04:49Z"}]}
 {"id":"ft-akx00.5","title":"[EPIC] Replace core runtime, scrollback, recording, and FTUI placeholders","description":"Own audited placeholders in core runtime state, scrollback access, recording, and FTUI migration seams.

These items sit close to the product core: if they stay fake, higher-level workflow, UI, and persistence work inherits weak assumptions and missing behavior.","design":"Sequence shared state first, presentation second:
- wire real scrollback/state access,
- decide whether dormant encoding variants should be implemented or removed,
- replace FTUI placeholders only after the underlying data surfaces are trustworthy,
- clean up stale stub annotations so comments match reality.","acceptance_criteria":"- No audited core-runtime placeholder remains.
- FTUI surfaces are backed by live data instead of placeholder text.
- Recording/state code either implements advertised variants or removes them.
- Comments in policy/state code accurately describe reality.
- Every concrete child bead under this epic closes with comprehensive unit tests plus a dedicated or explicitly assigned E2E/integration scenario, all executed under the shared verification contract in `ft-akx00.8`.","notes":"Primary files: `crates/frankenterm-core/src/fleet_scrollback_coordinator.rs`, `crates/frankenterm-core/src/recording.rs`, `crates/frankenterm-core/src/tui/ftui_stub.rs`, `crates/frankenterm-core/src/policy.rs`.

## Track Verification Emphasis
- state/scrollback/replay/FTUI verification with snapshot fixtures, replay traces, and structured UI-state logs.
- Heavy validation runs in this track must capture structured logs, exact command transcripts, and artifact paths.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:28:57.180371Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-03T02:04:29.995468Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["core","e2e-tests","ftui","integration-tests","placeholder-remediation","quality-gate","stub-audit","test-logging","user-impact"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-akx00.5","depends_on_id":"ft-akx00","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:28:57.180371Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2745,"issue_id":"ft-akx00.5","author":"jemanuel","text":"This track is where placeholder removal becomes architecture work rather than text cleanup. The goal is to stabilize the underlying state model so UI and workflow surfaces inherit something real.","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:28:57Z"}]}
 {"id":"ft-akx00.5.1","title":"Replace NullPaneScrollbackAccess with real pane scrollback wiring","description":"`crates/frankenterm-core/src/fleet_scrollback_coordinator.rs` still uses `NullPaneScrollbackAccess` as an explicit placeholder “before real pane scrollback storage is wired in”.

This task owns wiring the coordinator to real pane scrollback data so fleet-level features stop depending on a no-op adapter.","design":"1. Trace the scrollback producer/consumer boundary and choose the authoritative storage/access interface.
2. Replace the null object with a real adapter backed by current pane/session state.
3. Ensure read behavior is bounded and compatible with future large-scrollback work.
4. Add tests proving coordinator features observe real data rather than placeholder emptiness.","acceptance_criteria":"- `NullPaneScrollbackAccess` is removed from production wiring or relegated to clearly test-only/debug-only status.
- Fleet scrollback coordination reads real pane data.
- Tests verify normal, empty, and error-path behavior with real adapters.
- Comments no longer describe production wiring as temporary placeholder logic.
- Comprehensive unit tests cover the happy path, edge/boundary cases, explicit error/failure/cancellation modes, and repeat-run stability relevant to this bead.
- A dedicated E2E/integration or explicitly named higher-level suite exercises the user-facing or integration-facing surface touched by this bead, with structured logs, artifacts, and exact execution commands captured under the shared contract in `ft-akx00.8`.","notes":"This is a prerequisite for any higher-level FTUI or workflow feature that wants trustworthy historical pane content.
